demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Maven compiler plugin version to a newer snapshot release.
Change the Maven compiler release property from Java 17 to Java 21.
demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.why_exists: The project targets Java 21, so the compiler must be configured to generate bytecode compatible with Java 21; otherwise, it will compile for Java 17.

demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.general_idea: Add explicit release configuration to the maven-compiler-plugin configuration block.
demo-backendservice1/pom.xml.why_exists: Ensures the plugin explicitly uses Java 21 as the release target, reinforcing the property change.

demo-service1/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Maven compiler plugin version to a snapshot release compatible with Java 21.
Change the Maven compiler release property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK version.
demo-service1/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would compile against Java 17 APIs, failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing runtime issues if Java 21-specific APIs are used.

demo-service1/pom.xml.general_idea: Explicitly set the release configuration in the maven-compiler-plugin to 21.
demo-service1/pom.xml.why_exists: Ensures the compiler plugin explicitly targets Java 21, overriding any default or inherited settings that might still point to Java 17.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler source and target properties to Java 21 to align with the JDK migration.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 17 language features and bytecode, failing to utilize or comply with Java 21 requirements.
